scene: A moviegoer stick figure at a ticket window handing one dollar bill to a cashier stick figure, small sign reading TICKETS above the window.
The same dollar bill flying out of a small theater building toward a tall building labeled STUDIO, a long red arrow tracing the money leaving.
A grinning STUDIO suit stick figure clutching a fat stack of cash, while a sad theater-owner stick figure holds one tiny coin labeled 5 cents.
A stick figure scratching his round head, a big red question mark floating above him.
The sad theater-owner stick figure standing in front of a blank movie screen, both pants pockets turned inside out, small label 0 dollars.
A small theater building with an OPEN sign on the door and a giant red question mark hovering over the roof.
The moviegoer stick figure sitting in a cinema seat with a popcorn bucket on his lap, a red arrow pointing down at the bucket.
One giant popcorn bucket centered on the frame, red and white stripes, labeled POPCORN, the hero object.
A single movie ticket with a worried frowning face drawn on it, a red arrow pointing at it.
The STUDIO suit stick figure holding up a clipboard contract labeled TERMS, pointing sternly with one finger.
The same contract paper now stamped with the word BRUTAL and a small skull doodle, the theater-owner stick figure sweating beside it.
A pie chart split with the big slice colored for STUDIO, next to a calendar page labeled WEEK 1-2.
A stack of movie tickets turning into cash, a red arrow carrying the cash to the STUDIO suit stick figure.
A small bar growing taller step by step under a horizontal arrow labeled TIME, the theater-owner stick figure starting to smile.
A cinema marquee sign reading SOME MOVIE YOU FORGOT with a few cobwebs in the corners.
A theater door labeled SCREEN 9 with a small sign WEEK 6, one lonely stick figure sitting inside.
The theater-owner stick figure finally smiling, holding a few coins, small dollar symbols floating around his head.
A ticket price tag rising with an up arrow, but 70 cents of a dollar floats away toward the STUDIO building, label 70 cents.
The theater-owner stick figure handing a dollar bill back to the STUDIO suit stick figure, a red arrow showing it return.
The theater-owner stick figure buried under a stack of labeled boxes reading RENT, STAFF, and AC.
A film projector with a huge price tag, drawn bigger than a tiny house beside it, label with three dollar signs.
A pie chart with only a tiny sliver shaded, labeled 4%, the theater-owner stick figure squinting at it.
A smug vending machine with a little face beating the sad theater-owner stick figure, a VS between them.
The theater-owner stick figure in a safari hat holding a magnifying glass, hunting, a red arrow pointing off to the side.
A popcorn bucket being filled, with a small price tag reading 50 cents and a cheap bag of kernels beside it.
A few tiny kernels on the left with an arrow labeled x20 pointing to a big fluffy pile of popcorn on the right.
A squeeze bottle labeled BUTTER squirting a yellow stream onto a popcorn bucket.
The hero popcorn bucket with a big price tag reading 9 dollars, the moviegoer stick figure handing over cash.
The popcorn bucket with a handwritten price tag reading exactly 1,275%, a big red arrow pointing at the tag, the moviegoer stick figure wide-eyed in shock.
The moviegoer stick figure shrugging with both palms up, boxed in by walls, a big red question mark over his head.
A stick figure trapped inside a dark black box room, a clock on the wall showing 2:00, one lonely snack counter beside him.
A sign reading NO OUTSIDE FOOD with a crossed-out sandwich, a guard stick figure standing next to it with arms crossed.
A waiter stick figure with an obviously fake smile, a big red X drawn over him.
A tycoon stick figure in a top hat and monocle standing in a theater lobby, label MONOPOLY above him.
A pie chart almost completely shaded, labeled 85%, the hero popcorn bucket sitting next to it.
A stick figure shaking his head no, holding up an official-looking document.
A document labeled AMC FILINGS with a big handwritten number 800M next to a popcorn bucket and a soda cup.
The same AMC document, now showing SALES 800M on top and COST 150M below it.
An open ledger book labeled 81% with the hero popcorn bucket sitting on top of the pages.
A simple outline map of the United States with a giant popcorn bucket standing on top of it.
A stick figure flipping a horizontal line upside down, a curving red arrow showing the flip.
A pie chart with one small slice shaded, labeled CONCESSIONS 20%.
A second pie chart with a nearly half slice shaded, labeled PROFIT 50%, a red arrow contrasting it with the small slice.
Split frame: on the left a movie screen with a full row of seated stick figures, on the right the popcorn bucket handing over an envelope labeled RENT.
An old theater-owner stick figure with a long gray beard, a small framed family photo on the wall behind him.
A calendar or sign reading 1908, the old bearded owner stick figure pointing at it.
The old owner stick figure literally trading a film reel in one hand for a popcorn bucket in the other, two arrows swapping them.
A stick figure with one finger held to his lips going shh, a small label SECRET beside him.
A popcorn bucket with a heartbeat pulse line running across it, keeping a small theater building labeled ALIVE.
A fancy theater entrance with a sign showing a crossed-out popcorn, label NO POPCORN, small tag 100 YEARS AGO.
A snooty owner stick figure in a top hat with his nose in the air, a chandelier above and a patterned carpet below.
A row of snobby stick figures wearing top hats and monocles.
A popcorn bucket with stink wavy lines rising off it and CRUNCH noise marks, a few kernels spilled on the floor.
A big NO POPCORN sign with a crossed-out popcorn bucket, the top-hat owner stick figure pointing firmly to the door.
A street popcorn cart with a vendor stick figure parked on the sidewalk outside a theater building.
A coat-check counter with a popcorn bucket hanging on a hook like a jacket, a numbered ticket stub, sign COAT CHECK.
A glum crowd of stick figures with empty turned-out pockets, a big downward red arrow, label THE DEPRESSION.
A stick figure stepping through a glowing movie screen used as a doorway escape, holding a small ticket.
A small popcorn bag with a price tag reading 5 cents, a hand holding up a single nickel coin.
A theater-owner stick figure gulping nervously, then a small popcorn stand pops up beside him with an OPEN sign, he looks relieved.
A fancy theater building with a CLOSED sign and a tombstone in front reading RIP.
A proud female stick figure labeled JULIA BRADEN standing tall holding a popcorn bucket.
Julia stick figure with a speech bubble talking her way past a theater door, label KANSAS CITY on a small map pin.
Julia stick figure standing on top of a tall pile of popcorn buckets like an empire, small fancy theaters around her stamped BROKE.
A map pin labeled DALLAS, a grid of many small theaters each with a tiny popcorn machine, big label 80.
Five fancy top-hat theaters refusing a popcorn machine with a NO gesture, big label 5.
A snobby fancy theater with its nose in the air over a crossed-out popcorn bucket, label TOO CLASSY.
A crowd of 80 small smiling theaters with dollar symbols and an up arrow, label 80 THRIVING.
Five fancy theaters with sad faces dripping red dollar signs like bleeding money, a down arrow, label 5.
A SUGAR sack with a ration stamp, a crossed-out candy bar and a crossed-out soda cup, label WWII.
A popcorn bucket with little legs walking in, wearing a crown and sitting on a throne, label NO SUGAR.
A pie chart with more than half shaded as popcorn, labeled OVER 50%, small tag 1945.
A movie theater building with one giant popcorn bucket filling its inside.
A stick figure holding up a blueprint scroll labeled THE BUSINESS.
A fishing hook with a tiny film reel as bait next to a mouse-trap with a popcorn bucket on it, labels BAIT and TRAP.
A theater building with buttery smell wavy lines pumping out of its vents, a big nose sniffing toward it.
A winding dotted arrow path on the lobby floor leading a stick figure right past the popcorn counter.
An engineer stick figure in a hard hat with a blueprint and gears, pointing at the popcorn counter.
A stick figure walking through a theater door, a small film reel drawn as a doormat at the entrance.
The moviegoer stick figure frowning at a popcorn bucket with a 9 dollar tag, a grumble cloud over his head.
The popcorn bucket with a label OVERPRICED crossed out by a big red X.
The moviegoer handing a popcorn bucket that morphs into a RENT envelope to the now-happy theater-owner stick figure.
The moviegoer stick figure looking shocked as a movie ticket falls from his hand, a big handwritten stamp reads YOU GOT PLAYED, the hero popcorn bucket winking.
